rationale: The citing paper uses Spiller et al. (2013) in its empirical results section to justify and implement floodlight-style probing of interaction effects. Specifically, the authors state they conducted floodlight analyses to test simple effects of direct and indirect channel usage across moderator levels and then interpret reversals/disordinal patterns based on those probes. This is a functional methodological use in analysis and interpretation, not a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies Spiller et al.'s moderated regression probing approach (floodlight/simple-effects testing) to interpret its interaction terms. It uses that framework to identify where the effects of channel usage differ across levels of governance mechanisms.
